exercises.title: PROPER CASE
promptLines: Define **capitalize(s: string): string** with the first character uppercase and the rest lowercase. Empty string -> ''.

exercises.title: TALLY GLYPH
exercises.brief: Count how many times one glyph appears.
promptLines: Define **countChar(s: string, ch: string): number** returning how many times `ch` occurs in `s`. Match case exactly.
starterLines: function countChar(s: string, ch: string): number {
solutionLines: function countChar(s: string, ch: string): number {
exercises.hint: Loop the characters of s and do n++ each time c === ch.
exercises.lore: Every matching glyph trips the counter once.

exercises.title: LETTER MIRROR
exercises.brief: Do two strings hold the very same letters?
promptLines: Define **isAnagram(a: string, b: string): boolean** that is `true` when both strings contain exactly the same characters (the same counts), regardless of order. Compare case-sensitively. Sorting each string's characters and comparing the results is the simplest route.
starterLines: function isAnagram(a: string, b: string): boolean {
solutionLines: function isAnagram(a: string, b: string): boolean {
exercises.hint: Sort the characters of each string and compare: x.split('').sort().join('').
exercises.lore: Same atoms, rearranged — the mirror still recognises them.

exercises.title: DOMINANT SIGNAL
exercises.brief: Find the character that shows up the most.
promptLines: Define **mostFrequentChar(s: string): string** returning the single character that occurs most often, ignoring spaces. On a tie, return the character that *first* reaches that maximum count. Assume `s` has at least one non-space character.
exercises.hint: Tally each non-space char in a map; update best only when a count strictly beats the current max.
exercises.lore: Amid the chatter, one signal climbs loudest first.

exercises.title: SHIFT CIPHER
exercises.brief: Rotate the lowercase alphabet forward, wrapping past z.
promptLines: Define **caesar(s: string, n: number): string** that shifts every lowercase letter `a`-`z` forward by `n` positions, wrapping `z` back to `a`. Leave all other characters (uppercase, spaces, punctuation) unchanged.
Use char codes: `'a'` is 97. For a letter, `((code - 97 + n) % 26) + 97` gives the shifted code.
starterLines: function caesar(s: string, n: number): string {
solutionLines: function caesar(s: string, n: number): string {
exercises.hint: Loop chars; only when code is 97..122 rebuild it as ((code - 97 + n) % 26) + 97, else keep c.
exercises.lore: Slide the glyphs down the ring; whatever falls off z reappears at a.
